title: Eva and jungian archetypes
wikitext:
[]: Eva and jungian archetypes             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Mon Jan 26, 2009 4:36 pm I was interested in connecting up Eva characters to Jungian archetypes. Unfortunately, there are a bunch that I can't classify, and some that might be disputed. Here's my attempted correlation. Shinji - Child Fuyustuki - Senex Kaji - Puer Aeternus Misato - Artist-Scientist Kaworu - Self Asuka - Trickster Obviously, these are up for argument. Correlating Asuka to trickster is simply because she doesn't follow the rules. I'm arguing that Misato is the Artist-Scientist because that involves actually seeming innocent about the world (which Ritsuko does not) and having an intuition about solutions. The argument for correlating Kaworu with Self is mostly because he seems quite whole and at peace in comparison to other characters. The other correlations should be pretty self-explanitory.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Jan 26, 2009 4:57 pm well ain't this funny coincidence as I'm currently engaged in conv. about Jungian aspects of Evangelion elsewhere... I'll propably make topic for end results - quite fascinating. What could be most awesome about it is we could finally let the superficial Freudism crap rest in peace (OMG Shinji WANTS TO BONE HIS MOTHER LULZ LULZ LULZ), as "freudisms" can be explained by Jungian aspects. I had never before realized some suspiciously Jungian smelling stuff in the show; now they seem obvious Self isn't archetype exactly. Self is just Self. Rest is...well, sounds like someone with some really basic and simple grasp of Jungianism applied archetypes to human personality stereotypes and named their funkily. Jung always maintained there won't be all compassing personality types or dream types since everyone goes through their own, very specific Individualization process. Granted, I haven't even heard of "Senex" or then I can't connect that to any concept I've heard of. So, could you tell what these mean and where did you dig up these personality types (NOT Archetypes, as those are general building blocks of psyche and are SUBCONSCIOUS)?

[]:             Quote Post by Enki v.2 » Mon Jan 26, 2009 5:16 pm Senex is the official term for the "sage" or "wise old man". I was relying on wikipedia, since my grasp of jungian psychology is, as you said, cursory and minimal. I was looking for the archetype that Rei matches to, which I'm aware of, but I can't find anything that resembles it on wikipedia or in my book on introductory jungian psychology. Robert Anton Wilson calls it "the silver lady", and says it correlates with the moon, water, and motherhood (!!!).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jan 26, 2009 5:19 pm You may be interested in this: http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=203533

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Jan 26, 2009 5:40 pm Enki v.2 wrote: Senex is the official term for the "sage" or "wise old man". I was relying on wikipedia, since my grasp of jungian psychology is, as you said, cursory and minimal. I was looking for the archetype that Rei matches to, which I'm aware of, but I can't find anything that resembles it on wikipedia or in my book on introductory jungian psychology. Robert Anton Wilson calls it "the silver lady", and says it correlates with the moon, water, and motherhood (!!!).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jan 27, 2009 10:29 pm Ornette wrote: You may be interested in this: http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=203533

[]: Re: Eva and jungian archetypes             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Jan 28, 2009 2:59 am Enki v.2 wrote: Shinji - Child Fuyustuki - Senex Kaji - Puer Aeternus Misato - Artist-Scientist Kaworu - Self Asuka - Trickster
